w:r.w:t: Representative dashboard concept · Prepared by Operi · May 29, 2026

All figures in this package are illustrative and were generated to demonstrate Operi’s dashboard design, data integration, KPI reporting, and multi-organization performance measurement capabilities. The data does not represent actual Regional Catalyst Network, the regional university system, or partner-organization records. This concept is provided to help evaluators visualize the proposed future-state platform.
This folder contains everything needed to stand up the ecosystem dashboard in Power BI Desktop and to reproduce the look of the interactive concept mockup.
All-in-one data model (4 tables on separate tabs). Recommended import source.
Monthly fact table — 480 rows across 20 partners × 24 months.
Partner organization directory (type, county, city, source).
Data inventory / quality audit for the Data Quality page.
Power BI report theme — Operi dark navy / electric-blue palette.
One-click connector that opens Power BI pointed at this folder.
Interactive 7-page mockup (open in any browser) — the visual target.
Make sure Power BI Desktop is installed (free from the Microsoft Store).
Power BI opens and connects to this folder. In the Navigator, choose the four files (or the workbook) and click Load / Transform Data.
If Windows opens the .pbids with a text editor instead, right-click it → Open with → Power BI Desktop.
Open Power BI Desktop → Home → Get data.
In the Navigator, tick the four tables: Fact_Ecosystem_Monthly, Dim_Partners, Data_Sources, Data_Dictionary → Load.
View tab → Themes → Browse for themes.
. The canvas, cards, and charts adopt the dark navy / electric-blue palette automatically.
Set each page background to #07111F (Format pane → Canvas background → Color, 0% transparency) for the full command-center look.
paste the DimDate script (bottom of DAX_Measures.txt) via Modeling → New table.
Fact_Ecosystem_Monthly[Reporting Month] → DimDate[Month]; Fact[Organization Name] → Dim_Partners[Organization Name]; Fact[Data Source] → Data_Sources[Source Name]. All single-direction, one-to-many.
select DimDate → Table tools → Mark as date table → [Date].
Modeling → New measure, then paste each measure from DAX_Measures.txt.
Build a persistent left sidebar so the report reads like a unified platform rather than separate report tabs:
Insert → Shapes → Rectangle down the left edge (fill #06101D) on every page, or build it once and copy to all pages.
Insert → Buttons → Blank for each of the 7 pages; label them and add the matching icon (Insert → Image).
Select a button → Format → Action → Type = Page navigation → Destination = the target page.
Alternatively use Insert → Buttons → Navigator → Page navigator for an auto-updating menu.
Add the Operi wordmark (Insert → Image, operi-logo) at the top of the sidebar with the subtitle “Powered by Operi”.
Place a small text box “Representative concept · sample data” in the header or footer of every page.
Page order matches the seven sections in the concept mockup. Each table lists the visuals and the exact fields to drop in.
[Businesses Supported], [New Businesses Started], [Jobs Created], [Capital Raised], [Active Partner Orgs], [Referrals Made]
Location = County; Bubble size = [Businesses Supported]; Color = [Capital Raised]
Axis = Organization Name; Values = [New Businesses Started], [Jobs Created]
Reporting Month, County, Industry Sector, Partner Type
[Entrepreneurs Served], [Jobs Created], [Jobs Retained], [Programs Delivered], [Support to Start Rate]
Axis = Month; Column = [Businesses Supported]; Line = [New Businesses Started]
Rows = County; Values = supported / started / jobs / capital
Rows = Sector; Columns = County; Values = [Businesses Supported]
X = [Businesses Supported]; Y = [Capital Raised]; Size = [Jobs Created]; Legend = Industry Sector
Axis = Partner Type; Values = served / started / jobs
Org, Type, Served, Referrals, Started, Jobs, Capital, [Avg Data Completeness] — add conditional formatting (color scale) on key columns
Axis = Month; Values = served / funding / started
Decomposition tree or custom Sankey visual
Axis = Entrepreneur Demographic Group; Values = [Entrepreneurs Served]
[Capital Raised], [Grants Awarded], [Loans Facilitated], [Avg Capital per Business], Funding Success Rate
Location = County; Bubble = [Jobs Created]; Color = [Capital Raised]
Axis = County; Values = [Jobs Created], [New Businesses Started]
Axis = County; Legend = Industry Sector; Values = [Businesses Supported]
Jobs / retained / started / capital / entrepreneurs by county
[Total Data Sources], [Avg Data Completeness], Sources needing cleanup, Ready for automation
Axis = Source Name; Values = Completeness Score (color: ≥90 green, ≥70 amber, else red)
Legend = Status; Values = count of sources
Source, Owner, Format, Frequency, Last Updated, Completeness, Status tag, Issues
Metric, Definition, Source, Calculation Logic, Refresh Frequency
File → Export → Export to PDF to attach static pages to the proposal, or
Publish to the Power BI Service for an interactive evaluator link, or
Open Operi_Ecosystem_Dashboard_Concept.html in a browser and use Print → Save as PDF for an immediate, fully-rendered visual.
Questions or refinements to the concept can be incorporated quickly — the data model, theme, and measures are fully parameterized.
